extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo won Madrid sprint with 1km Quick-Step lead-out, taking 4th stage but lost green to FROOME who contested intermediate sprint
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step executed 1km lead-out on Madrid circuit, demonstrating team sprint train still viable at race-end fatigue levels.
Froome/Sky contested intermediate sprint specifically to defend green jersey from Trentin, prioritizing jersey over ceremonial stage protocol.
Three-man breakaway (Ciolek, De Marchi, Schultz) survived 20km on Madrid circuits before peloton reeled them in final lap.

rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: Won 4th stage matching best Vuelta sprint haul since Degenkolb 2014, showed consistent fast-twitch preservation across three weeks
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Won Vuelta by 2min margin (largest in 10 years), contested intermediate sprint despite overall lead showing Sky's jersey-defense obsessiveness
rider_signals.DE MARCHI Alessandro: neutral: Labeled 'never tiring' for breakaway effort on final stage, consistent with pattern of late-race animation
